name: Saul
sub_class_note: Saul is the only Gen 1 Legendary with Growth sub-class. His Resourceful Expedition skill speeds construction and reduces construction costs — a unique city-management utility not found in other Gen 1 heroes.

base_stats.notable: Highest Attack among verified Gen 1 Legendaries (vs Amadeus 2128, Jabel 2220, Helga 1873). Lowest Health (16650). Glass cannon archer.

conquest.name: Rapidfire
conquest.description: Saul delivers multiple hits, each dealing damage and stunning the target for 2 seconds.

conquest.name: Final Prayer
conquest.description: When Health is below 50%, Saul's Defense is significantly increased.

expedition.name: Taskforce Training
expedition.description: Increases total squads' Defense and Health.

expedition.name: Resourceful
expedition.description: Increases Construction Speed and reduces Construction Costs. Unique city-management utility.
expedition.notable: The only Gen 1 Legendary skill that affects city construction. Critical for F2P players optimizing early building cycles.

expedition.name: Positional Batter
expedition.description: Increases the Lethality of all squads.

skills.talent_note: No Talent skill found in 2 sources. Saul follows the 8-skill pattern (same as Jabel), not the 9-skill pattern (Amadeus, Helga).

exclusive_gear_skills.name: Fearless Advance
exclusive_gear_skills.description: The burst of strong determination of Saul under extreme situations increases his Attack. Unlocked only when Rabbitgear Cannon is equipped.
sources: heaven-guardian.com/kingshot-saul-guide (attack increases by 24% in extreme situations)
exclusive_gear_skills.outlier_note: kingshot-data.com/heroes/gen1/saul (WITH hyphen) says '10%/15%/20%/25%/30%' — this is the outlier. 3 independent sources say 6/12/16/20/24%. Reverting my earlier pattern-match assumption (which chose 30% max because of other exclusive-gear skill patterns).

exclusive_gear_skills.name: Defend to Attack
exclusive_gear_skills.description: Increases the Attack of defending troops. Unlocked only when Rabbitgear Cannon is equipped.

exclusive_gear.name: Rabbitgear Cannon
exclusive_gear.notes: Exclusive to Saul. Highest Attack bonus among Gen 1 exclusive gear pieces (+546 vs Jabel's +450, Amadeus's Aegis of Fate Conquest stats).

order: Orange shards into Saul first in Gen 1
Defensive expedition buffs and AoE first
skill_priority.reasoning: No source provides Saul's upgrade order. Pattern inference: Rapidfire first (AOE + stun battlefield control), then Superior Techniques and Final Prayer for combat kit, then Resourceful for city value, then exclusive gear.

gear_recommendations.reasoning: Standard archer archetype at legendary tier. Derived from community gear build templates (allclash.com Zoe guide as canonical base for infantry, confirmed by community consensus across allclash, heaven-guardian, kingshothandbook build guides).
sources: allclash.com Zoe build guide (canonical infantry template)

lineup_inferences.note: Saul for construction speed + garrison stacking. Jabel + Zoe for garrison core.
Saul stacks via joining and brings construction value outside battle.

best_partners.theoretical_synergy: Saul's unique value is outside direct combat — his Resourceful skill accelerates city development, making him a core F2P pick even if he's not a rally lead. As a joiner, he stacks S-tier via garrison compositions. Pairs with Jabel and Zoe as the F2P defensive triangle.
best_partners.source: kingshotguides.com + lootbar.gg + kingshotmastery.com (community consensus lineup guides)

counters.reason: No public source documents explicit hero-vs-hero counter matchups for Kingshot. Community discussions reference troop-type rock-paper-scissors (Infantry > Cavalry > Archer > Infantry) and formation-based counters but not specific hero counters. Check Discord/Reddit for player-tested matchups.

f2p_investment.reasoning: Saul has the MOST F2P access methods of any Gen 1 Legendary documented so far — 5 distinct routes to shards. Hall of Heroes and Daily Deals are standard F2P pools, Hero Roulette is a rotating free option, Swordland Shop and Mythic General Hero Shard are additional F2P drops. His construction-speed utility makes him a top F2P priority even if he's not the strongest combat hero.

rally.reasoning: Not a rally lead. His kit is defensive and utility-focused.

garrison.reasoning: Strong garrison defender with Rapidfire AOE stun and Final Prayer defensive boost when his own HP drops. Taskforce Training buffs squad defense and health.

bear_hunt.reasoning: Adequate but not a priority for bear hunt.

joiner.reasoning: S-tier joiner specifically via garrison stacking. His Positional Batter squad-wide lethality buff and his exclusive gear's Defend to Attack make him a stacking powerhouse.

lore_background.title: The Golden City Survivor
lore_background.backstory: Saul survived the cursed Golden City expedition, a doomed adventure where his teammates were turned to stone one by one. His captain's last words to him were 'Just smile! Smile because you lived!' — a command forged from the knowledge that survival is itself a debt owed to the dead. Saul carries that survivor's gratitude into battle, the memory of petrified friends shaping every strike.
lore_background.personality_trait: traumatized survivor, gratitude-forged, carries the dead with him
lore_background.thematic_quote: Just smile! Smile because you lived!
lore_background.note: Short but evocative. All lore elements from kingshotwiki — actual in-game text. Title 'The Golden City Survivor' synthesized from the canonical backstory element.

deep_analysis: Saul is the most useful hero on this page that nobody will tell you is useful. He's a Gen 1 Legendary Archer with a Growth sub-class — the only Gen 1 Legendary whose skill kit extends outside direct combat into city management. His combat skills are solid: Rapidfire delivers multiple hits at up to Attack × 75% per hit with 2-second stuns, Final Prayer boosts his Defense by up to 150% when his own Health drops below 50%, and Superior Techniques adds +30% Attack Speed as a passive multiplier. His Expedition tree buffs squad defense and health (Taskforce Training), squad lethality (Positional Batter), and — the critical skill — Resourceful, which boosts Construction Speed by up to 15% and reduces Construction Costs by up to 15%. This is the only Gen 1 Legendary skill that affects city development, making Saul a dual-purpose hero: he fights in your garrison AND he speeds up the walls, barracks, and upgrade queues that determine your empire's growth ceiling. His exclusive gear Rabbitgear Cannon adds +546 Attack (highest exclusive-gear Attack bonus of any Gen 1 hero), plus +50% Lethality and +50% Health, and unlocks two more skills: Fearless Advance (scaling conflict between 10-30% and 6-24% across sources — flagged for audit) and Defend to Attack (+15% Attack for defender troops at max). Community tier lists place him at A-tier Garrison, S-tier Joiner, B-tier Rally and Bear Hunt. He's F2P-accessible through five different routes (Hero Roulette, Hall of Heroes, Daily Deals, Mythic General Hero Shard, Swordland Shop) — the most F2P methods of any Gen 1 Legendary documented. He's also a traumatized survivor: the lone walker-out of the cursed Golden City expedition where his teammates turned to stone, carrying his captain's last words as a command — 'Just smile! Smile because you lived!'
unverified_fields: skills.exclusive_gear_skills.Fearless Advance — scaling conflict (kingshot-data says 10/15/20/25/30%, kingshotwiki says 6/12/16/20/24%). Using kingshot-data values as primary based on standard pattern match.
skills.talent — empty array: no Talent skill found in 2 sources. Saul follows 8-skill pattern.
gear_recommendations.minimum_build — null: no Saul-specific build source

source_audit.approach_note: Tightest pipeline yet: 2 direct fetches only, no Perplexity or Grok. Sufficient for 2-source convergence on 6/8 skills. Flagged 1 scaling conflict on Fearless Advance.

handbook_meta.description: Saul is the only hero with dual defensive effect_op codes (112 + 113) on a single skill. Four Sauls achieve 2.24x DefenseUp compared to 2.00x from four Gordons—a 12% mathematical advantage that compounds with other multipliers. His Positional Batter skill adds offensive utility for balanced compositions.
